description: India's dream of homeownership faces a reality check in 2026 as property prices and rents surge. Millions of urban Indians are now weighing the financial implications of buying versus renting. While buying offers long-term security, renting might be the smarter move for many amidst rising costs and shrinking affordable housing supply.

title: Age of software export controls starts with a bug report as US treats AI model like a missile
description: The United States has expanded export controls to cover advanced AI models. This means access to powerful AI is now treated like a physical export. Companies like Anthropic must restrict access for foreign nationals. This action signifies a major shift in how technology is regulated. It suggests AI is now considered strategic infrastructure, not just software.

Longer than World War I: 10 ways Russia-Ukraine conflict has changed the world for decades to come
description: The Russia-Ukraine war, now longer than WWI, is fundamentally reshaping global order. It has triggered NATO expansion, a European arms race, and elevated drone warfare. Russia faces deep dependency on China, while North Korea and Iran profit as key arms suppliers. Beijing learns from Moscow's failures, and India navigates US pressure over Russian oil.

Peter Thiel's secret Dialog society 'exposed' in data leak: Who's on the list
description: A data leak has exposed members of Dialog, the secretive invitation-only society co-founded by Peter Thiel in 2006. First surfaced by hacktivist maia arson crimew, the 222-name list for its 2026 retreat includes NATO's top Europe commander, US senators, Treasury secretary Scott Bessent, Elon Musk and Stanford president Jonathan Levin. The records reveal off-the-record sessions on WWIII and cult-building, a members' dating app, and leaked political-leaning data.

description: Rapar police have arrested an Imam from Ajmer for allegedly facilitating the illegal religious conversion and Nikah of a 17-year-old girl. The girl's family reported her abduction by Jahin Abdul Chauhan, who allegedly assaulted her and forced her into marriage after conversion. Both accused are in police custody.

Crissy Froyd claims Mike Silver liked old bikini photos after Russini Story Row
description: Crissy Froyd has accused The Athletic's Mike Silver of liking old bikini photos on her Instagram immediately after she published a column about the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal, alleging the behaviour was targeted. Froyd, who was previously fired from USA Today Sports over her criticism of Russini, has alleged that at least six female reporters admitted to her that they traded intimacy for NFL access. Meanwhile, Russini has resigned from The Athletic, which is now conducting an internal investigation.

title: No victors, only losers: How Iran war left every side worse off
Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
description: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: Microsoft president Brad Smith has a message for students booing tech CEOs: I agree with you, but…
description: Microsoft president Brad Smith has responded to graduating students who booed AI at commencement ceremonies this spring, calling the backlash a "powerful wake-up call for the tech sector." In a 3,000-word essay, Smith acknowledged the class of 2026 faces a "perfect storm" of AI automation and tech layoffs, yet urged graduates to adapt rather than fear AI. His optimistic message arrives as Sam Altman, Mustafa Suleyman, and Dario Amodei clash over AI's job impact.
